source.title: New Cafe Setup for 2025 (Y10S2 rework) - How To Defend Cafe Dostoyevsky ALL Sites - Rainbow Six Siege
source.notes: Auto-generated captions (ASR), presenter has a heavy cold/hay fever which adds noise. Generic ranked setups, not pro-league lineups, per the creator's own framing. Room/callout names are the creator's informal terms. A few operator names are garbled by the ASR and are flagged inline below rather than guessed as a specific real operator: 'zombie' (used 3x for a shield-carrying anchor, e.g. 'a zombie shield here and here'), 'as army'/'as aramis', and 'a bulletproof with sentry'. 'Keepa barrier' is treated as a likely reference to Kapkan's EDD given the trap/denial context, but flagged for verification. Compass grounding: Ubisoft's official blueprint zip for this map (same source used for Bank) contains only unlabeled, low-resolution floor overlays with no compass rose or room labels legible at the available resolution, so compass directions could not be reliably assigned for this file — locations below are room/feature names only.

reinforcements.location: Two walls at top red (near Piano)
reinforcements.note: Reinforce both. Prioritize denial gadgets here — few attackers bring EMP for this spot.
Reinforce this side, head holes on the far side. Holds both windows plus the cigar double door with a safe escape back into bathroom.
Always reinforce unless running a mirror setup.

reinforcements.location: Wall facing new hatch area (optional, paired with freezer walls)
reinforcements.note: Blocks anyone swinging out from new hatch from shooting straight through.
Reinforce; pair with a rotate on the right side of the same wall.

headHoles.note: Kill hole for anyone crossing the punch-hole sightline.
Make a second hole next to the first but don't peek from it — hide in the unused one for a safer trade.
Alternate setup: reinforce the windows side instead and head-hole here. Peeks both windows plus toward the reinforced side; retreat into bathroom crosses the head holes.
Stand on the desk/bar to see toward the cigar shop double door. Pair with a punch hole for the same sightline.

headHoles.location: Cocktail bar, punch hole through the bar/door
headHoles.note: Punch (don't shotgun, too loud) all the way through to hold that door too.

rotates.location: Top White wall, LEFT side (as viewed from the room)
rotates.note: Left side only — right side gets peeked from the skylight.
Pairs with the left-side reinforcement; sneak crouched into cocktail behind the bar instead of using the door.

hatches.note: Listen at round start for how attackers open these. DMR/hard breach/shotgun/battering ram: no action needed. Breaching charge or sledge: run to the second hatch and throw C4 for a free kill the moment they open it. Don't overcommit past the first ~90 seconds.
Reinforce if anchoring in this room (operator unconfirmed — see source notes on 'keepa barrier').

gadgetPlacements.location: Top red drone hole / jammer spots
gadgetPlacements.note: Jam the drone hole into piano/top red; multiple good jammer spots in this area.
Place a barrier-type gadget here plus one on the side of the stage — covers the windows, lets you peek out onto top red. Verify against the video before relying on this.

gadgetPlacements.location: Shield anchor behind the sofa/seat, near the windows
gadgetPlacements.note: Couple of ADS shots behind the sofa doesn't miss.
Hide a camera in the bright spot near the bar — catches attackers pushing toward pixel/bathroom after opening the red wall.

gadgetPlacements.location: Floor beneath cupboard / small angle nook near cocktail
gadgetPlacements.note: Second camera spot. Floor here is soft to the marble — expect and use frags from below.

gadgetPlacements.location: Trash feet holes (bottom of the wall near the bin bags)
gadgetPlacements.note: Reveals anyone dropping new hatch and moving to plant behind trash, behind the long bar, or at the default spot. Never reinforce these walls instead.

operatorRecommendations.note: Jams the top red drone hole and general area around piano/cocktail.
Runs both cams; gets frequent C4-from-below kills on the soft floor near cocktail.

operatorRecommendations.holdLocation: Behind the Sofa, Near the Windows
operatorRecommendations.note: Does not match a real operator name — treat as unconfirmed shield-carrying anchor (context suggests something like Azami given her Kiba Barrier, but this is a guess) until verified against the video.

generalTips.note: Shoot both the speakers and the clock at round start to stop the map's ambient sound cues.
Shoot the glass out of the windows even if you leave the barricade — clears sound so you can hear repels.
Watch every window on this floor — extremely window-dense site.
Bring an extended barrel on the MPX (or similar).

reinforcements.note: Reinforce; pair with feet holes across the bottom.
Reinforce — otherwise attackers throw a hard breach charge from the window onto this wall.

reinforcements.location: Two doors/walls toward bakery from the K1/K2 island area
Hatch above bottom white + two walls for bottom white
reinforcements.note: Reinforce the hatch and both walls.

headHoles.note: Leave soft with head holes only if coat check is manned (shoot the radio, play from there). If coat check isn't manned, reinforce this side instead.

headHoles.location: Wall across the top of the far kitchen half
headHoles.note: For throwing C4s/smokes to the other side of the site and peeking toward prep/behind the bomb.

rotates.location: Small rotate near the bakery counter (optional)
rotates.note: Lets a bakery-counter defender get back across site. Situational.

gadgetPlacements.location: Prep window / feet holes across the bottom
gadgetPlacements.note: See attackers entering the bakery door before they see you. Avoid peeking the bakery door itself — heavy attacker cover outside (van, car, bakery counter angle).

gadgetPlacements.location: Feet holes behind coat check / long bar area — TWO shots' worth only
gadgetPlacements.note: Keep these short (about two shotgun blasts). Longer lets attackers prone at the double door and see three-quarters of the site plus prep window.

gadgetPlacements.location: Shotgun hole under the train window
gadgetPlacements.note: Holds the jump-in spot from underneath when attackers repel/jump through the train window above.

operatorRecommendations.note: Jammers behind the door and around coat check; commit to holding this room with the bottom-red wall left open.

generalTips.note: Two main attacker approaches into this site: bakery and the bottom-right side.
K1 is the first kitchen island reached coming through prep, K2 is the second.
Default plant spot when attackers push bakery is tucked behind the bomb near coat check — hold from the coat check side to contest it.
Don't peek toward long bar too much — multiple angles attackers could be holding from (door, behind the bar/half wall, by the tables).
Don't Castle-barricade the long bar approach — gives attackers free roam on the other side.
Bottom white has a fully soft ceiling and floor — roam/hold above from train museum/fireplace or attackers open the floor.
Watch for an upside-down repel angle on the train window that sees a long row of the site.
Holding coat check is mandatory if the bottom-red-right head holes plan is used.
Keep a roaming presence upstairs (piano/cocktail) — don't cede train museum/fireplace uncontested from the start.

reinforcements.note: Reinforce both. Optionally leave one side soft for the first ~30-50 seconds to contest pillar for drone kills, then reinforce it.
Reinforce both fully — no rotate here.

headHoles.location: Fireplace, top wall near the hatch (into white stairs)
headHoles.note: Covers anyone entering via the door or the fireplace itself, seen from white stairs.

headHoles.location: Extra hole right next to the hatch
headHoles.note: Covers attackers who repel into the fireplace window and tuck into the corner to plant; walk down the stairs and peek that corner safely.

rotates.note: Rotate toward laundry; keep laundry hatch open.
Pass in and out without being seen from the window.

hatches.note: Leave open for the upstairs vertical plan.

gadgetPlacements.note: Barricade at round start; add a proximity/trap gadget around this door.

gadgetPlacements.location: Fireplace, all along the bottom of the room
gadgetPlacements.note: Feet holes along the entire bottom wall so a defender in reading can see through into fireplace.

gadgetPlacements.location: Reading room floor, vertical angles into reading/laundry/pillar below
gadgetPlacements.note: Barricade this door, the reading door below, and the piano/pixel door. Reinforce everything else off, hold upstairs. Make vertical floor holes down into the reading door, laundry door, and over the laundry wall into pillar below.

operatorRecommendations.note: Full castle setup upstairs denying doors below plus vertical floor holes.

generalTips.note: Feet holes needed across this site — Tachanka's kit makes them easier than Smoke's.
Upstairs floor is soft in the same places as the floor below, over hard floor beneath that — no risk of being shot from below while holding upstairs.

If attackers drop through the vertical/floor holes and plant behind the bookcase near the reading room door, hold with two players — one watching the rotate, one watching the site door — while watching for a flank from behind.

sites.floor: Ground Floor (with Top Floor piano/cocktail component)

reinforcements.location: Two red walls at top of red (upstairs, near piano)
reinforcements.note: Reinforce both — same top red walls used in the Top Four site plan.

reinforcements.location: Pixel Door Wall, Behind Hold Side
reinforcements.note: Reinforce this side, head holes on the far side — reverse of the Top Four site setup. Falls back down white protected the whole way.
Reinforce — this is the wall most attackers fire through when dropping red or new hatch.

headHoles.location: Down white, same head holes as the Fireplace/Reading site
headHoles.note: Covers fireplace plus the plant-corner angle.
Peek around the door side, fall back down white behind the reinforcement.

rotates.location: Downstairs site, tight to the left of a wall
rotates.note: Peek onto the nearby window and retreat.

rotates.location: Fireplace rotate (reused from the Fireplace/Reading site)
rotates.note: Same rotate spot as the other site.

hatches.note: Leave OPEN this time (opposite of Top Four site) — lets a defender drop into the site to help if the downstairs fight is lost.
Open as an escape route from the top floor if needed.

gadgetPlacements.note: Same unconfirmed shield-anchor setup referenced for the Top Four site — one shield-carrying defender on each of two spots in piano; source audio unclear on exact operator name. Hold piano over cocktail — most of the site sits under piano.

gadgetPlacements.location: Downstairs site, two walls with feet holes
gadgetPlacements.note: Reinforce both walls and add feet holes to see anyone entering train through the double door, or prone to see the far door.

gadgetPlacements.location: Fireplace, one wall's worth of feet holes
gadgetPlacements.note: Lighter than the dedicated Fireplace/Reading site — mainly to catch a late plant/rotate.

Floor is soft in specific spots — good for C4-from-below. Second operator name unclear in source audio ('a Valkair or a bulletproof with sentry') — treat as unconfirmed until checked against the video.

operatorRecommendations.note: Cameras plus exploiting the soft floor sections downstairs.

generalTips.note: Considered the weakest of the four sites, still playable.
Requires a hybrid approach: part upstairs piano/cocktail defense, part downstairs fireplace/train museum defense.
Attackers can dart between repel spots at the corner in about 5 seconds since the repel changes.
Use furniture/exhibits and train carriages in train museum as cover; shoot noisy exhibits to silence false audio cues.
No one plays inside fireplace itself on this site either — unsafe due to the window.
